title: Attributes and __init__
summary: Initialize object state with constructor parameters.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Attributes and __init__
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, classes are blueprints for creating objects. The `__init__` method is a special method called when an object is created. It allows you to initialize the object's attributes with values. For example:

```python
class Dog:
    def __init__(self, name, age):
        self.name = name
        self.age = age

my_dog = Dog('Buddy', 3)
print(my_dog.name)  # Output: Buddy
print(my_dog.age)   # Output: 3
```

In this example, we created a `Dog` class with `name` and `age` attributes. The `__init__` method sets these attributes when we create a new `Dog` object. 

**Your turn:** Try creating your own class with an `__init__` method!

quizDraft.title: Create a Class with Attributes
quizDraft.prompt: Define a class called `Car` that has attributes for `make`, `model`, and `year`. Implement the `__init__` method to initialize these attributes.
quizDraft.starterCode:
class Car:
    def __init__(self, make, model, year):
        # Initialize attributes here
        pass

# Create an instance of Car
my_car = Car('Toyota', 'Corolla', 2020)
print(my_car.make)
print(my_car.model)
print(my_car.year)
quizDraft.solutionCode:
class Car:
    def __init__(self, make, model, year):
        self.make = make
        self.model = model
        self.year = year

my_car = Car('Toyota', 'Corolla', 2020)
print(my_car.make)
print(my_car.model)
print(my_car.year)

quizDraft.title: Add a Method to Your Class
quizDraft.prompt: Add a method called `car_info` to the `Car` class that returns a string with the car's details.
quizDraft.starterCode:
class Car:
    def __init__(self, make, model, year):
        self.make = make
        self.model = model
        self.year = year

    # Add car_info method here
    pass

my_car = Car('Toyota', 'Corolla', 2020)
print(my_car.car_info())

quizDraft.title: Modify Your Class
quizDraft.prompt: Modify the `Car` class to include a method that updates the car's model.
quizDraft.starterCode:
class Car:
    def __init__(self, make, model, year):
        self.make = make
        self.model = model
        self.year = year

    def update_model(self, new_model):
        # Update the model here
        pass

my_car = Car('Toyota', 'Corolla', 2020)
my_car.update_model('Camry')
print(my_car.model)

quizDraft.title: Understanding Class Attributes
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following statements about class attributes are true? Select all that apply.
options: Attributes are defined inside the class using the __init__ method.
Attributes can be accessed using the dot notation.
Attributes are unique to each instance of the class.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how attributes are created and accessed in a class.

help.concept: Class attributes are defined in the __init__ method and can be accessed using dot notation. They can be of any data type and are typically unique to each instance.
help.hint_1: Attributes are not limited to strings.
help.hint_2: Consider how you create and access attributes in your class.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the Blank
quizDraft.prompt: The __init__ method is also known as the __________ method.
quizDraft.template: The __init__ method is also known as the __________ method.
